meaning: n.<主苏格兰>烟,水蒸汽,雾,臭气vi.冒烟,发臭气,散发vt.用烟熏,散发
examples: He couldn't see the hyena, but the stench of it was there, the stagnant reek of meat and sweat and piss.
Build succeeds when tests reek (or fail).
"Trophy desks reek of success," Dawson says.
On the last Sunday of July (Reek Sunday), devout pilgrims follow his original route, traditionally without shoes.
There is no reek of nostalgia about it, no sense that Mr Heaney is repeating himself.
